title: system tests: enable skopeo REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE
body:
skopeo pr #829 adds REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE support; this lets us
enable the following test:

  podman login - shares credentials with skopeo - via envariable

(I seriously doubt that the CI VMs have been updated with the
new skopeo, but I can leave this PR in limbo until that happens.
Otherwise I'll forget to enable the test).

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: This is almost certainly going to fail CI. That's ok and expected. We can just let this sit until the VMs are updated with new skopeo.
No idea. Rebased and force-pushed, we'll find out soon.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.

Well, it passes in `test fedora-32`:
```
[+0223s] ok 55 podman login - shares credentials with skopeo - via envariable
```
...but I still have no idea what will happen when CI is reenabled on more platforms; and I would hate for this test to fail just because the Ubuntu or F31 VM has an old Skopeo. I'm OK with leaving this PR in limbo until CI is back.
@cevich, which version of Skopeo do we run in the Ubuntu VMs?

> which version of Skopeo do we run in the Ubuntu VMs?

Hrmmm, is it not listed in the `package_versions` task ... damn, those tests are disabled :confounded: I can fire up a VM with `hack/get_ci_vm.sh` and take a peek...

```
root@cevich-ubuntu-19-libpod-6224667180531712:/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod# contrib/cirrus/logcollector.sh packages
Ubuntu 19.10 \n \l

Kernel:  5.3.0-1011-gcp
Cgroups:  tmpfs
conmon-2.0.15~1-amd64
containernetworking-plugins-0.8.5~75-amd64
containers-common-0.2.0~1-all
cri-o-runc-1.0.0-3~dev2-amd64
criu-3.13-2ppa1.19.10-amd64
golang-2:1.12~1ubuntu1-amd64
libseccomp2-2.4.1-0ubuntu0.19.10.3-amd64
podman-1.9.0~2-amd64
skopeo-0.2.0~1-amd64
slirp4netns-1.0.0~1-amd64
```

Good 'nuff or need Ubuntu 18 also?

>skopeo-0.2.0~1-amd64

I'm having trouble reading the skopeo git log, but as best I can tell v0.2.0 includes the required fix.

> CI VMs have been updated with the new skopeo, but I can leave this PR in limbo until that happens.

I've been regularly building new VM images (which automatically grab updated packages at build time) in PR #6201 and #6193 (in case either picked up the desired version): Check their `.cirrus.yml` for the new `_BUILT_IMAGE_SUFFIX` value.  If not, and you want to try rolling new VM images, the [process is documented](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/cirrus/README.md#test_build_cache_images_task-task) and (relatively) pain-free (for a simple package update).
